description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white and blue, featuring the ANA airline livery. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under its low-mounted, swept-back wings, which are equipped with subtle winglets at the tips. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible along the side for passengers. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of modern wide-body airliners used for medium to long-haul flights.
description: A large ANA-liveried commercial airplane with low, swept-back wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is white and blue with a forward cockpit and passenger windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ANA livery, two underwing engines, and the full length of the fuselage and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail fin, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, engines, and tail, with clear visibility of the wing sweep.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, and the wide wingspan with both engines visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with subtle winglets and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle (not visible, inferred from typical configuration)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
